game_info.title: Night Watch - Anomaly Detection
game_info.concept:
As an employee of a mysterious organization your job is to monitor live surveillance camera footage and spot anomalies using your sharp eyes and good memory. Can you survive the entire night shift or will you succumb into paranoia?

I'm on observation duty is a short horror game where your job is to monitor live surveillance camera footage and spot anomalies in the monitored rooms. Anomalies range from furniture movement to otherworldly intruders. When you spot an anomaly all you have to do is file an anomaly report describing the type and location of the anomaly. Simple, right? You will need sharp eyes and good memory to survive the entire night. Are you up for the task? Maybe you'll even discover some secrets along the way.
game_info.description: You are a security monitor working the night shift for a mysterious organization. Your task is to watch multiple surveillance camera feeds and identify anomalies - objects that move, change, appear, or disappear between camera views. Report anomalies by selecting the correct room and anomaly type before time runs out. Survive until 6 AM by catching enough anomalies while avoiding false reports. Missing too many anomalies or filing incorrect reports will lead to termination. Stay vigilant and trust your memory.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between cameras and UI elements
Space: Select/Confirm camera or report option
Shift: Toggle report menu
Z: Quick switch to previous camera
Enter: Start game
Escape: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic camera navigation and anomaly detection system. Verifying that the player can successfully switch between cameras and identify simple anomalies like furniture movements.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through all available cameras systematically using arrow keys. When an anomaly is detected, open the report menu with Shift and file reports for visible anomalies. Focus on catching furniture movement anomalies first as they are the most common.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test successfully navigates all cameras, detects at least 5 anomalies, and files correct reports. The game progresses through multiple time periods without game over. Test passes if anomaly detection rate is above 60%.
